headline: Apple's post-Cook future hinges on whether Ternus can ignite AI growth
summary: Apple's leadership change is the start of a new phase for the iPhone maker, in which the focus will be on whether its deep ‌hardware heritage can be successfully fused with AI to sustain growth in a fast-evolving ‌tech landscape, Wall Street analysts said.  Apple faces mounting AI‑driven competition and pressure from the Trump administration to bring more manufacturing back ​home.  Investors would be keen to see how CEO John Ternus navigates Washington's changing policies, an area that was deftly managed by his predecessor Tim Cook.

headline: Apple Needs a New Era of Innovation as CEO Cook Announces Departure
summary: Apple  announced on Monday night that Tim Cook will be stepping down as CEO—and the company needs a new era of innovation to begin with his departure.  The company said in a news release that Cook will be exiting his position as chief executive officer of the tech giant effective Sept. 1.  Cook, 65, will become executive chairman of  Apple’s  board of directors and John Ternus, currently Apple’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will become the new CEO.

headline: Tim Cook Became a Billionaire During His Apple Tenure

headline: Apple's Ternus era hints at renewed hardware focus, AI growth push
summary: Apple's appointment of longtime hardware chief John Ternus as CEO signals a renewed emphasis on its core strength in devices ‌and focus on fusing AI capabilities into existing products to sustain growth, analysts said.  Ternus, a ‌25-year veteran who has overseen development of key products including multiple generations of iPhone, will take the helm in September, succeeding ​Tim Cook after more than a decade of strong growth that saw Apple's market value swell to around $4 trillion.  While Apple has faced investor concerns over the pace of its generative AI rollout and ceded its position as the world's most valuable company to Nvidia, Wall Street analysts said the leadership change shows that its ‌existing hardware ecosystem remains central to its ⁠growth story.

While Apple has faced investor concerns over the pace of its generative AI rollout and ceded its position as the world's most valuable company to Nvidia, Wall Street analysts said the leadership change shows that its ‌existing hardware ecosystem remains central to its ⁠growth story.

headline: Tech Weekly: Tim Cook steps aside, robots run marathon
summary:
STORY: From a huge Apple exit to robots running marathons... this is Tech Weekly.

:: Tech Weekly

Tim Cook announced he will leave his job as Apple CEO later this year.

Stock prices have soared 20-fold since Cook took over as CEO in August 2011.

Apple's longtime hardware boss John Ternus is due to replace him.

The firm said Ternus will take charge from September 1, while Cook stays on as executive chairman.

////

A reusable booster of the New Glenn rocket launched from Florida this week touched down successfully after about 10 minutes.

However, Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin rocket failed to deploy the AST SpaceMobile communications satellite it was carrying into the correct orbit.

AST said the BlueBird 7 satellite was placed into a lower than planned orbit by the upper stage of the launch vehicle.

The ⁠satellite was part of an effort to build a space-based ​cellular broadband network, similar to Amazon's Leo or SpaceX's Starlink.

///

Robots outrun people at this year’s Beijing half-marathon, beating the human winners by more than 10 minutes.

The robots raced alongside 12,000 men and women, running in a parallel track to avoid collisions.

The winning machine, developed by Chinese smartphone brand Honor, finished the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

Nearly half of the robots ran the 13-mile race autonomously, while others were remotely controlled.

Although some of the machines impressed more than others.

///

Tesla's supervised full self-driving - or FSD - was seen on the streets of Amsterdam.

It followed surprise approval from the Netherlands for its system.

It's a European first for city streets and could lead to more near autonomous vehicles on the continent.

Although it has split opinion - with some cyclists and politician cautious about it, while supporters believe it could lead to safer driving.

"To be able to experience it firsthand here in the Netherlands, that's unreal, because I've been waiting for this moment ever since Autonomy Day in 2019, where (Tesla CEO) Elon (Musk) said this is going to come, and the moment's finally here.''

///

And Netflix saw a big exit as Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago.

He transformed the firm from a DVDs-by-mail business to a global streaming giant.

It comes as Netflix looks for new avenues of growth as sales slow due to competition..

.

The firm also forecast earnings per share in the current quarter below analyst expectations.

headline: Apple taps hardware veteran Ternus to succeed Cook as CEO
summary:
STORY: :: Apple

Apple on Monday named longtime hardware boss John Ternus as its next CEO.

In a statement, Apple said the senior vice president of hardware engineering will take over September 1 while Tim Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

Ternus, who joined Apple in 2001, has played a central role in reviving products such as the Mac, which has gained market share against PCs.

Though he has kept a low public profile, he has been deeply involved in shaping Apple's biggest products such as iPads and AirPods.

Apple shares fell around half a percent in extended trade after the news broke. 

:: Apple

Stock prices have soared 20-fold since Cook took over as CEO in August 2011.

He boosted Apple's market value by $3.6 trillion in his 15 years at the helm.

Cook also made his early reputation at Apple by building out its sprawling supply chain with contract manufacturers in China.

:: Apple

That meant expensive factory operations and product inventories were mostly kept off Apple's books while maximizing profits.

The leadership change comes at a crucial time for Apple.

It was dethroned by AI chipmaker Nvidia as the most valuable company, as investors have fretted over its lack of innovation in the technology.

Integrating AI into the iPhone - the most successful consumer product in history - may be Ternus' hardest challenge.

Apple helped introduce AI to the public in 2011 with Siri.

But more than a decade later, it has yet to produce AI related hits while emerging rivals like OpenAI’s ChatGPT have drawn hundreds of millions of users.

headline: Omdia: India’s smartphone shipments fell 5% in 1Q26 amid channel caution and pricing pressures
summary: LONDON, April 21, 2026--The latest Omdia research shows that India’s smartphone shipments fell by 5% year on year to 30.9 million units in 1Q26, reflecting seasonally weak demand compounded by cautious channel inventory strategies. Demand was pressured by macro headwinds, including rupee depreciation and rising inflation weighing on affordability and delayed consumer upgrades. Additionally, earlier front-loading ahead of expected price increases limited incremental channel intake.

headline: Apple's Tim Cook will still be around to help with China
summary: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced on Monday that he plans to step down as chief executive role, effective September 1, and transition to an executive chairman role. Cook will be succeeded by Apple senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus. LightShed Partners partner and TMT analyst Walter Piecyk comes on Morning Brief to discuss what this change may mean for Apple's mission and the iPhone maker's relationship with China.
